subtaskTitle: Understand the rulings on maintaining ties with non-Muslim relatives

payload.sentence:
Study the hadith of Asma bint Abi Bakr, who asked the Prophet whether she should maintain ties with her polytheist mother — he said yes.
3.

resolved.note: Recorded in Sahih al-Bukhari 789 (Volume 3) and 407 (Volume 4); also found in Sahih Muslim 2194 and 2195.

subtaskTitle: When a relative falls ill, visit promptly and ask how you can help practically

payload.sentence: Visiting the sick is one of the five rights of a Muslim over another Muslim, as stated by the Prophet (peace be upon him).

resolved.note: While 'visiting the sick' is recorded as one of seven commands in Sahih al-Bukhari 331 and 625, the specific 'five rights' formulation is not present in the provided excerpts.

subtaskTitle: Designate a clean, quiet corner or room as the household prayer area

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: "Make some of your prayers in your houses and do not turn them into graves" (Bukhari and Muslim).

resolved.note: Found in Sahih al-Bukhari 424 (Volume 1) and 280 (Volume 2), as well as Sahih Muslim 1703 and 1704.

subtaskTitle: Remove wall art depicting animate beings — replace with calligraphy or nature imagery

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) instructed that images of animate beings (humans and animals with distinct features) not be hung on walls, as the angels do not enter a house containing such images (Bukhari).

resolved.note: Recorded in Sahih al-Bukhari 318 (Volume 3), stating that angels do not enter a house containing pictures; also mentioned in hadiths 447, 539, and 338.
